sops.title: Accounting- Pulling the correct Inventory Reports
sops.body:
1
Navigate to Data
From the home screen, go to the 'Reporting' module on the left sidebar, then select 'Data'.
__Notes:__ This section allows you to view and export raw data from Fishbowl.
2
Find Inventory On Hand
![](/uploads/sops/accounting-pulling-the-correct-inventory-reports/image-1.jpeg)
In the 'Data' section, locate and select either 'Inventory On Hand (FBA)' or 'Inventory On Hand (Main)' from the list. IF you pull the Inventory On Hand (All) report YOU MUST DUMMY CHECK to make sure your Inventory Availability report is also pulling both warehouses.
__Notes:__ Choose the appropriate option based on whether you need to view Amazon FBA inventory or main warehouse inventory.
3
Export Data
![](/uploads/sops/accounting-pulling-the-correct-inventory-reports/image-2.jpeg)
Click the export icon to export the inventory data to a file.
__Notes:__ You can export the data in various formats, such as Excel.
4
Navigate to Reports
Next, go to the 'Reporting' module and select 'Reports'.
5
Find Inventory Availability
![](/uploads/sops/accounting-pulling-the-correct-inventory-reports/image-3.png)
In the 'Reports' section, find and select 'Inventory Availability'.
6
Modify Report
![](/uploads/sops/accounting-pulling-the-correct-inventory-reports/image-4.png)
Click 'Modify Report' to customize the report by filtering by location group or other criteria. IF you pull the Inventory On Hand (All) report YOU MUST DUMMY CHECK to make sure your Inventory Availability report is also pulling both warehouses. *It would only pull both warehouses IF the Location Group is Blank in modify reports.*
__Notes:__ This step is technically optional but allows you to narrow down the report to specific locations that would match the location you pulled on your Inventory on hand Report.
7
Save Report
Click 'Save' to save the report in various formats such as PDF, CSV, or Excel.
__Notes:__ Choose the desired file format and location to save the report.
8
Do a Dummy Check
![](/uploads/sops/accounting-pulling-the-correct-inventory-reports/image-5.png)
Choose a product to check from your reports as a dummy Check (example- ESCA). Go to the 'Materials' module and select 'Inventory'.
__Notes:__ This section allows you to search for specific parts and view their inventory details.
9
Search for Product and confirm quantities PER LOCATION
![](/uploads/sops/accounting-pulling-the-correct-inventory-reports/image-6.png)
Enter the part number or description in the search bar to find the desired item. From there you can see how the breakdown of quantity is per location. Compare the numbers to your Inventory availability Spreadsheet in reports to confirm what warehouse or warehouses are included on the report. *In This example, I can see that ESCA has 42 Items in Amazon FBA and 48 Items in Main. Since my Inventory Availability Report is showing ESCA as having 90 Items. I can conclusively say that my report is pulling from both locations.*
__Notes:__ The system will display the inventory details for the selected part, including on-hand quantities, committed quantities, and available quantities.

sops.title: How Process an Item Return
sops.body:
## Purpose

Occasionally a customer will want to return an unopened product. It’s important to follow the correct process to ensure the accounting team, warehouse team, and customer are all taken proper care of.

## Scope

Customer Care

## Procedure

## Shippo

- 1. Ensure the customer hasn’t opened the bag of seeds, we can’t return opened seeds. 
-  __Locate the shipment in Shippo:__ Search for the customer’s original shipment by name, and confirm the shipment date aligns with their order date.
- Click their shipment > Navigate to the 3 dot bar in the bottom right > click on Create return label. From there you should get an option to download the return label, occasionally you may have to refresh the page and find their shipment again to download the label.
- Send the return label to the customer via email and await for the package to be sent back.

## Fishbowl

- Find the customer’s order in fishbowl. Sales Order > Search order number or customer name. (make sure status is set to all not All Open) 

![](/uploads/sops/how-to-process-an-item-return/image-1.png)

- Once you’ve pulled up the order, copy the SO number. Then, at the top of the window, click the dropdown arrow next to the __Duplicate__ button and select __Credit Return__. 

![](/uploads/sops/how-to-process-an-item-return/image-2.png)

- Once you’ve pulled up the order, copy the SO number. Then, at the top of the window, click the dropdown arrow next to the __Duplicate__ button and select __Credit Return__. Make sure the items in the order are listed as a Credit Return in the type box: 

![](/uploads/sops/how-to-process-an-item-return/image-3.png)

- Finally, click __Quick Fulfill.__ The customer’s order is now fully processed in Fishbowl, meaning the inventory is ready to be restocked in the warehouse and the financial records have been updated.

## Woocommerce

- Open the customer's order in WooCommerce.
- Towards the bottom left of the order, press the button that says "__Refund__" ![](/uploads/sops/how-to-process-an-item-return/image-4.png)  

- Some boxes should appear to the right of the products in the order screen. You can fill out the amount that you need to refund. 
- Click on the __Refund via Stripe button__ to complete the process. They will receive an email stating they are getting a refund for whatever amount you put in. KEEP IN MIND: Customers can read what you put in the refund reason box, so be nice. ![](/uploads/sops/how-to-process-an-item-return/image-5.png)

## Exceptions / Special Notes

Always inspect the returned order to ensure all products are present and remain unopened.

Customers are automatically sent an email when a refund is processed through woo. You don’t necessarily need to reach out to them after processing a refund, it’s up to your own discretion.

sops.title: Adding New Kit in Fishbowl
sops.body:
## Purpose

Biannually we add new Kits to the website, in addition to having them added into Woocommerce they need to be added into Fishbowl

## Scope

For whoever is in charge of channel cohesion and product management

## Procedure

1. Log into Fishbowl
2. Navigate to Materials > Product

If Adding Individual Kits: 

1. In the top right corner click on the down arrow next to the “New” Button
2. Select New Kit
3. From there add the following information in general
	1. Kit = Kit SKU
	2. Kit Disc. = Product Title
	3. Price = Matched price on the website
4. In Details: add Kit Weight (Total sum weight of all kit items)
5. In Kit Items: Click on the green \+ button on the far Right side 
	1. Kit Item Type = Standard
	2. Kit Item Product = Base product (CANNOT BE ANOTHER KIT)
	3. Qty = how many bags of base product
	4. Finish
6. Hit Save in the Top Left corner.

If adding bulk Kits:

1. Go to File > Export in the very top left corner
2. Select “Kit Items”
3. Hit Next > Finish
4. Open the Spreadsheet
5. Using existing Kit item layouts mirror the format and add new Kits to the bottom of the spreadsheet. Keep in mind that each new kit needs two rows.
6. Once all of the information is added in Save the spreadsheet
7. In Fishbowl Navigate back to File > Import
8. Select Kit Items then Next > Next
9. Add in the correct file and Finish
10. Double check in products that all of the new Kits came in correctly.

## Exceptions / Special Notes

This process is for new KITS only. New products  cannot be added in this manor, there is a whole other SOP for how to add new Products. NEW PRODUCTS HAVE TO BE ADDED FIRST BEFORE YOU ADD THEIR KITS. When you add in new kits they should automatically add into Sellware when it automatically pushes every two hours. So don't be discouraged if they are not imported immediately. If you need to manually push out of Fishbowl go to Integrations > Commerce > Product Export > Run Task. And it should load in the next 5 minutes on sellware.

sops.title: Moving Inventory in Fishbowl
sops.body:
## Purpose

You would move Inventory in Fishbowl for various reasons, when adding in new inventory, when needing to add inventory into Pick locations for orders.

## Scope

For the person in charge of daily inventory upkeep

## Procedure

List clear, step-by-step instructions for completing the process. Use numbered lists for clarity.

1. Log into Fishbowl
2. Navigate to Materials > Inventory
3. Type in your Desired SKU
4. Click into SKU Inventory page
5. Select the Location you want to change
6. Hit MOVE on the Right hand side
7. Confirm the quantity you're going to move
8. Select the new location
9. Hit Finish/Okay

## Exceptions / Special Notes

IF moving inventory to a pickable location for orders please make sure to write down the starting location and the ending location on a sticky note for the warehouse to find and relocate the product. 

New inventory that is added through a PO automatically gets placed in Main-Receiving and will need to be moved to the correct location.

sops.title: Receiving a PO in Fishbowl
sops.body:
## Purpose

Use this SOP when it is time to receive your PO from GS Purchase in Fishbowl

## Scope

For the person in charge of receiving and putting away new inventory

## Procedure

1. Go into your Purchase Order tab under Purchasing in Fishbowl
2. Click on the PO you wish to receive
3. Hit issue in the top Left corner of the screen
4. Select No when it asks if you want to send the email
5. Go into Receiving page
6. Click on the correct order
7. Click fulfill in the top left corner
8. From there input the Lot \# and Expiration date in for each product
9. Hit Finish once done
10. After Finished Navigate to Materials> Inventory 
11. Search up each product received
12. Move the correct quantity to the correct location as listed on the receiving sheet from the warehouse
13. Once everything is in the correct location you are done!

## Exceptions / Special Notes

New inventory that is added through a PO automatically gets placed in Main-Receiving and will need to be moved to the correct location.

sops.title: How to Use the Seasonal Planner
sops.body:
## What this tool is for

The **Seasonal Planning** page builds a full-season bulk order recommendation from three sources:

- **Historical sales** from last year's same season (Spring / Summer / Fall / Winter).
- **Current marketplace activity** — WooCommerce, Amazon, and Walmart pulled from the local sales cache.
- **Current Fishbowl inventory** from the most recent 10-minute snapshot.

It tells you, per SKU, how many lbs and how many bags to order to cover the upcoming season. From there you can save a plan and track real progress against it as POs land.

---

## Step-by-step: building a plan

1. Open **Seasonal Planning → Plan a Season** in the sidebar.
2. Pick the **Season** and **Year** you're planning for.
3. Pick a **Growth Rate**:
   - **Default (+3%)** matches the standard business assumption.
   - **Flat / +5% / +10% / -5% / -10%** apply a fixed multiplier to last year's same-season sales.
   - **Custom** lets you type any percentage if you're working off a specific business plan.
4. Click **Generate Seasonal Orders**. The forecast appears in four tabs:
   - **Combined Orders** — per-SKU forecast, current inventory, and net-to-order.
   - **Detailed Breakdown** — for mix SKUs, the per-component seed quantities.
   - **Total Seeds** — totals by Code / Species / Variety.
   - **Species Totals** — grouped by species with variety subtotals.

### Forecast math, in one sentence

> Net to Order (Lbs) = ( Last year's same-season sales in lbs × Growth Rate ) − Current Inventory in lbs.

For brand-new SKUs with no history for the target season, the tool falls back to **Spring-or-Fall comparison-season sales × seasonal ratio** so you still get a starting target. Those rows are highlighted yellow.

---

## Adjusting Net to Order manually

The two **Net to Order** columns (Lbs and Units) in the Combined Orders table are editable inputs.

- Click into either cell and type a new value.
- Editing **Lbs** auto-updates **Units** by dividing by the bag size (e.g. `PB-GAME-10-LB` → 10 lb/bag).
- Editing **Units** auto-updates **Lbs** by multiplying by the bag size.
- The Detailed Breakdown, Total Seeds, and Species Totals tabs **instantly recalculate** to match.
- Edited rows are highlighted green and show a reset icon next to the SKU — click it to revert that one SKU back to the forecast value.

This is the place to apply vendor-driven adjustments (minimums, lot sizes), known events that historical data wouldn't capture, or anything else where you know better than the model.

---

## Saving a plan

When the numbers look right, click **Save Plan**.

- A dialog asks for an optional **Label** (e.g. "Fall 2026 v1 — initial plan") and **Notes**.
- Saving freezes the per-SKU **Net to Order** (lbs + units), forecast, current inventory, *and* the seed-mix recipes **at that exact moment**.
- The plan gets a timestamp and shows up under **Seasonal Planning → Saved Plans**.

You can save as many versions as you like — the system never overwrites. Use the **star** to mark whichever version you're actively buying against.

### What "Net to Order" means (and what it doesn't)

The number you save and track against is the **Net to Order** — how many lbs/units you're committing to *buy* on top of inventory you already had at save time. It is **not** the total lbs you want available for the season.

> Total season goal = Inventory @ Save + Net to Order
> Done = Current Inventory + POs received since save ≥ Total season goal

---

## Tracking progress

Open any saved plan to see two layers of information:

### 1. Season Summary card

The headline story of last year vs this year vs where we stand:

- **Last Year Same-Season Sales** — total lbs across the SKUs in this plan, summed from last year's actual sales. This is the historical baseline the forecast was built on.
- **This Year's Demand Forecast** — `Last Year × (1 + growth rate)`. The total lbs the model expects us to sell this season.
- **Inventory @ Save** — total stock on hand across the plan's SKUs at the moment Save Plan was clicked. Frozen — never changes.
- **Net to Order** — total lbs we committed to buy. By design, this is the gap between the forecast and what we already had: `max(0, Forecast − Inv @ Save)`, summed per SKU.
- **Total Goal for Season** — `Inv @ Save + Net to Order`. This is how much we want available for the whole season, ignoring sell-through.

### 2. Plan Execution Status card

How the *buying* is going:

| Card | What it means |
|---|---|
| **SKUs Done** | SKUs where the Received lbs has reached the Net to Order lbs. |
| **In Progress** | At least one PO is received or on order, but Received hasn't reached Net to Order yet. |
| **Not Started** | Nothing received and nothing on order for this SKU yet. |
| **Overall Net to Order Received** | `Total Received ÷ Total Net to Order` across the whole plan. |

### Per-SKU columns

- **Status** — Done / In Progress / Not Started / No order needed.
- **Inv @ Save (Lbs)** — frozen inventory at save time.
- **Net to Order (Lbs / Units)** — what we committed to *buy on top of inventory* when saving.
- **Total Goal (Lbs)** — `Inv @ Save + Net to Order` for that SKU.
- **Received (Lbs)** — sum of PO line quantities (converted to lbs) for that SKU where the PO was **received in Fishbowl** on or after the save timestamp.
- **On Order (Lbs)** — PO lines that have been **issued but not yet received**. They show up here so you can see what's coming, but they don't count toward Done until they're received.
- **Current Inv (Lbs)** — most recent inventory snapshot. Already includes any received POs.
- **Progress bar** — `Received ÷ Net to Order`. 100% = we've received everything we said we'd buy.

A SKU is **Done** when `Received ≥ Net to Order` — i.e. we executed the buying commitment. Inventory changes after save are shown for context but don't move the progress bar.

---

## Where the data comes from

- **Last year's sales** → `sales_orders` + `order_line_items` in the local SQLite cache. SKU mappings are applied so consolidated SKUs (e.g. 5x5-lb → 25-lb) roll up correctly.
- **Current inventory** → `inventory_snapshots`, populated every 10 minutes by the auto-sync cron job.
- **Comparison-season sales (for new SKUs)** → same local sales cache, queried for the adjacent season.
- **Seasonal ratios** → calculated dynamically from `/api/seasonal-ratios` based on actual historical Fall-vs-Spring ratios per SKU prefix.
- **PO progress** → `purchase_order_lines` in the local cache. Receiving status comes from Fishbowl's `status_id` column (≥ 20 = received).

If something looks wrong, run `npm run sync` to refresh all caches.

---

## Tips & gotchas

- **Save before big changes.** Treat saved plans like checkpoints. Cheap to make, expensive to recreate from memory.
- **Re-saving doesn't update an old plan.** Each save creates a new timestamped version. That's by design — you can compare `v1 → v2 → v3` and see how your assumptions evolved.
- **Inventory is point-in-time.** If you save a plan and then 10,000 lbs gets received the next day, the progress view will credit it as Received Since Save — exactly what you want.
- **New-SKU forecasts are estimates.** Yellow-highlighted rows had no historical data for the target season, so the number leans on seasonal ratios. Override the Net to Order if you have better info.
- **Combined Sold (Lbs)** shows last year's actual same-season sales — useful for sanity-checking that the forecast isn't off by a multiple.

---

## Common questions

**Q: I edited Net to Order then re-ran Generate. My edits disappeared.**
Yes — clicking Generate refetches from the API and replaces the table. Save the plan first if you want to keep manual edits.

**Q: Why is "On Order" 0 even though I just created a PO?**
PO data syncs from Fishbowl periodically. If you just created it, give the next sync cycle a moment. If a PO was issued *before* the plan's save date, it's not counted — re-save the plan to capture the new baseline.

**Q: How do I unmark a plan as the "active" one?**
Click the star again to unstar it. Star whichever version you want progress tracked against most prominently.

**Q: Where is the seed-mix breakdown coming from?**
`/dashboard/mix-compositions` — the Combined Orders Net-to-Order drives the breakdown by multiplying by each mix component's percent.

body_structured.intro: End-to-end procedure for generating, shipping, and recording FBA replenishment shipments via Sellerboard, Fishbowl, and warehouse handoff.

phases.title: Build the Shipment Plan in Sellerboard

steps.text: Open Sellerboard and navigate to the Inventory Planner.
Review the recommended send quantities and select the products you want to ship.
Click Create Shipment Plan.
Adjust each product's send quantity to match a full-box multiple.
sub: Reference the Amazon Box Size Spreadsheet for units-per-box.
Rule: Only ship full boxes. Never send a partial box.

steps.text: Continue and double-check the packing groups Amazon assigns. Confirm products are grouped logically before moving on.
On the Box Content page, work through each product and assign:
sub: Box size (per Amazon Box Size Spreadsheet)

steps.text: Complete this for every product until all items show as "packed."

callout.text: Always pull box dimensions and weights directly from the Amazon Box Size Spreadsheet. Do not estimate.

steps.text: Select the ship date — use the same ship date for all products in the shipment.
Choose the Inbound Placement Service — pick the cheapest option available.
OPTION A — Small Parcel Delivery (SPD): use when shipment fits as individual boxes (no pallets).
sub: Set the delivery window approximately 1 week from the ship date.
Set the shipping mode — use the same mode for all shipments in the plan.

steps.text: OPTION B — LTL/FTL: use when shipping pallets of boxes.
sub: Do NOT book freight through Amazon's partner.
Book freight directly with Old Dominion.

steps.text: Review and Accept Charges.
Click Confirm Shipping.
Print Box Labels:
Once labels are printed, close out the shipment in Sellerboard.
Print all required:
In Fishbowl, navigate to Materials → Transfer Order.
The transfer order should auto-populate:
Set the type to "Move" — NOT "Shipping".
Mirror the total quantities from the Sellerboard shipment plan exactly.
Save the order and put it out for picking.

callout.text: Selecting "Shipping" instead of "Move" will route this incorrectly. Always verify before saving.

steps.text: Hand the following to the warehouse team:
Warehouse workflow: pick → sticker → pack → label → ship.
Picking is only finished when the shipment has physically shipped. Do not close picking early.
Once the shipment is processed in Fishbowl Shipping, inventory will automatically move and the transfer order will close.
No shipping information is required in the Fishbowl shipping module — it's a Move type, not a Ship type.

checklist: Sellerboard plan created from Inventory Planner recommendations
Quantities adjusted to full-box multiples only
Box content assigned (size, weight, qty) from spreadsheet
Same ship date set for all products
Correct shipping method chosen (SPD vs LTL/FTL)
UPS delivery window ~1 week out (if SPD)
Order released to warehouse for picking
Picking finalized only after physical ship

reminders.doIt: Use one ship date for all

sops.title: Editing Products in Sellware
sops.body:
## Purpose

Sometimes products do not sync properly from Fishbowl and you need to manually add or adjust them in Fishbowl Commerce (Sellware)

## Scope

For whoever is in charge of channel cohesion and product management

## Procedure

1. Log into Sellware (Fishbowl Commerce)
2. Go into Channels
3. Navigate on the Left to Products > All
4. Filter or find the sku you're wanting to edit
5. On the far right under actions- Click on the little edit Pencil
6. Once on the product page you can adjust: SKU, product title, product type, Pricing, and Kit Items (Plus more that shouldnt be necessary)
7. Once done hit save in the top right corner. DO NOT HIT PUBLISH

## Exceptions / Special Notes

These steps are for editing products that are already in the system. Not for new products. 

IF you need to edit a large quantity of products, at the top of the product list page there is an import/export button. Click on that and export your filtered products or all products and make the changes in there, then reupload the update spreadsheet in the SAME FORMAT. Or it will not transfer in correctly.

sops.title: Pushing Orders in Sellware
sops.body:
## Purpose

Sometimes an order will get stuck in Fishbowl Commerce (Sellware) due to various reasons including mismatched product. So we will have to go manually adjust and push through

## Scope

For whoever monitors orders and Fishbowl activity

## Procedure

1. Log into Fishbowl Commerce
2. Click into Channels
3. In the Left side menu option, navigate to order > draft
4. In draft (if there) choose a pending status order
5. Once viewing that Order page go through each item and confirm that the Listing SKU and the Product SKU match
6. If they do not or one SKU is missing input the correct corresponding SKU.
7. \*\* Take note of the quantity, price and tax amount before changing or adding the SKU \*\*
8. Once everything is added double check that the balance due is $0. 
9. If everything looks good Click “Save and Approve” on the Top Left of the page to issue it to fishbowl for fulfilment.
10. It will show up in fishbowl when the next order Sync happens (about every 10 minutes)

## Exceptions / Special Notes

Be sure to double check the original price and tax amount for each item before adjusting or adding the SKU. If they do not match up with what the order says on Woocommerce there will also be a discrepancy in Fishbowl. Accounting will come asking why theres a discrepancy. So avoid that and either write down what the starting amounts were, or have the order pulled up on Woocommerce to compare/match!

sops.title: Daily Cycle Counts
sops.body:
## Purpose

Daily Inventory counts to help minimize and identify inventory discrepancies in a timely manor

## Scope

Collaboration with the Warehouse and Warehouse supervisor

## Procedure

1. The warehouse conducts a Blind Inventory count using their cycle count worksheet. (Sharepoint: Warehouse Worksheets)
2. Worksheet is turned in before lunch so necessary adjustments are made while orders are not being processed.
3. In the Cycle Count Google Sheet, Match up the worksheet with the day and products/location: type in all of the blind counts
4. Switch over to Fishbowl
5. In Inventory look up each product and compare counts per location
6. Document any adjustments in the google sheet as you make them (EX: Fishbowl says 10, Blind count says 12. Cycle count \+2 to match up quantities)
7. If adjustments are needed in fishbowl: on the inventory page click “cycle count” on the right. Type in the new TOTAL Inventory amount for that location. 
8. Continue steps 5-7 for the rest of the products on the daily worksheet.

## Exceptions / Special Notes

IF counts are more than 5 bags off have the warehouse go recount. Or look into if anything is in receiving or shipping. Partially completed orders can make the counts look off. 

A Full cycle count occurs every 10 days. Before printing out a new cycle count set of worksheets double check that everything is up to date (Lot \#’s, Locations, etc) Changes to the worksheets are to be made in the Warehouse Worksheets Excel Workbook (IN Sharepoint).

A new Cycle Count Sheet needs to be added in the Cycle Count Google Sheet for every full cycle count. Account also uses this spreadsheet to keep track of adjustments.

sops.title: Order Picking/Packing Flow
sops.body:
## Purpose

Standard Procedure to pick, pack, and ship orders

## Scope

For our Warehouse associates and helpers to fulfill orders

## Procedure

1. Log into Fishbowl
2. Navigate to Sales > Pick/Picking
3. Click into The Sales Order
4. In the top options bar: hit Print and print to 4x6 label printer
5. Repeat steps 3&4 as many times necessary to get a group to go pick
6. Take the Pick lists and pick products, being sure to keep orders grouped and the pick list attached to each pile
7. Once Items are picked Hit Finish each order in Picking
8. Navigate to Shipping
9. Grab an orders products and match up the Sales Order Numbers 
10. Find a box that will fit the products snug with minimal empty space
11. In the Ship Details highlight the items you placed in the box/carton and click Add over to the right
12. Add the Box size dimensions and click okay/finish
13. Repeat steps 11&12 until all products are allocated in a carton
14. Once all products are in Cartons Click on Ship Express at the top and “Purchase a Label”
15. Always Select UPS Ground Shipping. If UPS Ground Shipping is not available talk to your supervisor before shipping
16. Purchase Labels and print
17. Tape up the box or boxes, stick the label on it and place it on a pallet for carrier pickup.

## Exceptions / Special Notes

If the Order is Red or Yellow in the pick list DO NOT PICK. Only pick orders that have a green status.

If UPS is not available or it is having an issue with shipping carriers first validate the address under ShipExpress > Validate address. If the issue still persists please connect with your supervisor so they can take a closer look.

If the shipping address is a PO Box please confirm with your supervisor before shipping. 

IF the order is more than 500lbs total DO NOT ship with a normal carrier. We Freight ship those orders. Let your supervisor know if an order is more than 500lbs.
